Milkshake Mania Wiki
Play
Wiki / Ingredients & supply chain

Ingredients & supply chain

Added in v1.3, the supply chain turns ingredients into an active resource you manage alongside money. It unlocks at Level 3 — before that, blends consume nothing. Once unlocked, every blend drains four ingredient types, and running dry on any one of them brings automated income to a complete halt until you restock (manual blending always works).

Ingredients

Four ingredients

Every shake — whether blended manually or produced by your automated staff — draws from four ingredient pools: Milk, Sugar, Fruit, and Ice. Each ingredient has its own capacity and drains at a fixed rate per shake blended.

Ingredient Consumed per shake Base capacity
Milk1.3 units600
Sugar1.1 units500
Fruit0.9 units450
Ice1.6 units750

Consumption can be reduced by the Efficient Kitchen Eternal upgrade. Capacity can be raised by purchasing capacity upgrades in the Supplies tab — each upgrade level adds 50% to every ingredient's base cap.

The penalty

Hard stop

If any ingredient reaches zero, all passive and automated income stops entirely — the income multiplier drops to zero — until you restock that ingredient. This replaced the earlier soft 40% penalty introduced at the start of v1.3, and it makes the supply chain something you actively monitor rather than occasionally top up.

Manual blending always works, even with empty supplies. You can still tap the blender and earn income by hand regardless of your stock levels, so you can never be soft-locked. If you run out of both cash and supplies, blend manually to recover — your hand-crafted shakes do not cost ingredients.

Dashboard

The Supplies dashboard

The Supplies tab gives you a live view of your entire supply chain from a single screen.

At the top, a supply-chain status banner shows whether you are currently stocked or running dry, so you can spot trouble at a glance. Below the banner, each ingredient displays:

  • Its current stock level and capacity.
  • The current drain rate (units per second), calculated from how many blends your automated staff are producing.
  • A time-to-empty estimate so you know exactly how long you have before the hard stop kicks in.

You can restock a single ingredient at any time by purchasing it individually, or use the Restock all one-tap button to top up every ingredient at once. Capacity upgrades — also purchasable from this tab — raise the cap for every ingredient type by 50% per upgrade level, giving you more headroom between restocks.

Management

Restocking

Because supplies drain faster than they restock passively, the supply chain is something you actively manage. There are two levers: buying stock directly from the Supplies tab, or assigning employees to the Restock role.

Each employee assigned to the Restock role regenerates 0.5 units per second across every ingredient type. Multiple Restock employees stack — two assigned workers regenerate 1.0 units/sec per ingredient, and so on. There is no automatic restock toggle separate from this: the Restock employee role is the intended hands-off solution, not a hidden setting.

Employee role allocation is managed from the Employees tab. You can assign roles manually with the steppers, use the one-shot Auto-assign button, or enable the Auto-Auto-Assign toggle to keep roles balanced automatically as your headcount changes (the standard policy targets roughly 25% Restock, 10% Management, and the remainder Production). See Employee roles for the full breakdown.

Two Eternal upgrades interact with the supply chain directly. Efficient Kitchen reduces ingredient consumption per shake, stretching your stock further. Stocked Pantry starts every new run with a full set of supplies so you never begin a rebrand scrambling to restock. Both upgrades are purchased with prestige tokens — see the Prestige & Eternal upgrades page for details.

Shops & upgradesMoney upgrades Employee rolesProduction, Management, Restock
